entities.name: WATER HYACINTH
entities.description: An invasive aquatic weed, Pontederia crassipes, known for rapid reproduction and nutrient depletion from water bodies.

entities.name: BIOENERGY
entities.description: Renewable and sustainable energy derived from biological resources.

entities.name: BIOECONOMY
entities.description: An economic system based on the sustainable use of biological resources.

entities.name: CIRCULAR ECONOMY
entities.description: An economic system aimed at eliminating waste and the continual use of resources.

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: Development that meets current needs without compromising future generations.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: Country where water hyacinths are abundant and circular bioeconomic challenges exist.

entities.name: LIGNOCELLULOSIC BIOMASS
entities.description: Plant-derived biomass consisting of cellulose, hemicellulose, and lignin, found in water hyacinth.

entities.name: BIOFUEL
entities.description: Fuel derived from biomass, such as water hyacinth.

entities.name: MANAGEMENT AND CONTROL STRATEGIES
entities.description: Approaches used to handle and limit the spread of invasive weeds.

entities.name: BIOMASS CONVERSION
entities.description: The process of converting biomass into energy or other useful products.

entities.name: WEED MINING METHODOLOGIES
entities.description: Unique methods for the successful collection, treatment, and long-term utilization of aquatic weeds.

entities.name: UN SUSTAINABLE DEVELOPMENT OBJECTIVES
entities.description: Global goals set by the United Nations for sustainable development.

entities.name: RAPID REPRODUCTION
entities.description: The ability of water hyacinth to multiply quickly, contributing to its invasive nature.

entities.name: NUTRIENT DEPLETION
entities.description: The capacity of water hyacinth to deplete nutrients from water bodies.

entities.name: ADAPTATION
entities.description: The ability of water hyacinth to adapt to new habitats.
